text: Vehicle classes under section 122 of the Motor Traffic Act as amended by Act No. 08 of 2009. A1 covers light motorcycles with engine capacity not exceeding 100cc; other class of vehicles that can be driven is G1; old class D. A covers motorcycles with engine capacity exceeding 100cc; other classes A1 and G1; old class D. B1 covers motor tricycles or vans with tare not exceeding 500kg and gross vehicle weight not exceeding 1000kg, including an invalid carriage; other class G1; old classes E and F. B covers dual purpose motor vehicles with gross vehicle weight not exceeding 3500kg and seating capacity not exceeding 9 seats inclusive of the driver's seat, which may be combined with a trailer with maximum authorized tare not exceeding 750kg; this class includes invalid carriages and cars with up to 9 seats inclusive of the driver's seat; other class G1; old classes C and C1.

C1 covers light motor lorries with gross vehicle weight exceeding 3500kg and not exceeding 17000kg, including motor ambulances and motor hearses, and may be combined with a trailer having maximum authorized tare not exceeding 750kg; other classes B and G1; old class B1. C covers motor lorries where gross vehicle weight is more than 1700kg and may be combined with a trailer having maximum authorized tare not exceeding 750kg; other classes C1, B, J, G and G1; old class B. CE covers heavy motor lorries and combinations of motor lorry and trailer including articulated vehicles where the trailer's maximum authorized tare exceeds 750kg and gross vehicle weight exceeds 3500kg; other classes C, C1, B, B1, G, G1 and J; old class B. D1 covers light motor coaches used for carriage of persons with seating capacity of not less than 9 seats and not more than 33 seats inclusive of the driver's seat and may be combined with a trailer having maximum authorized tare not exceeding 750kg; other classes C1, B, B1, G and G1; old class A1. D covers motor coaches where seating capacity does not exceed 33 seats inclusive of the driver's seat and may be combined with a trailer having maximum authorized tare not exceeding 750kg; other classes D1, C, C1, B, B1, G, G1 and J; old class A. DE covers heavy motor coaches, combinations of a motor coach with seating capacity of 33 seats inclusive of the driver's seat and a trailer with maximum authorized tare exceeding 750kg, or a combination of two motor coaches; other classes D, D1, C, C1, CE, B, B1, G, G1 and J; no old class is shown.

G1 covers hand tractors, meaning a two-wheel tractor with a trailer; no additional class of vehicles can be driven; old class G1. G covers land vehicles, meaning agricultural land vehicles with or without a trailer; other class G1; old class G. J covers special purpose vehicles used for construction, loading, and unloading, excluding motor lorries, light motor lorries, and heavy motor lorries, and equipped with construction equipment and equipment for loading and unloading goods; other class G1; no old class is shown.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka New Driving License
text: Instructions for obtaining the new smart card driving license. The page says licenses are issued in two methods. First method: online method at the Head Office for issue of licenses at Werahera and district offices in Kurunegala, Hambantota (printing available), Gampaha (including the Negombo sub office), Anuradhapura (printing available), Kalutara, Jaffna, and Monaragala. Second method: offline method at other district offices. Common requirements are that the applicant must be present in person and must bring the national identity card or a valid passport with the national identity card number. Where the online method is available, photographs are taken during the computer process and the applicant does not need to bring photographs. Where the offline method is used, two passport-size black and white photographs with a white background are required. For a first driving license, the original birth certificate must be produced.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka New Driving License
text: For obtaining a new driving license for light vehicles, the page states that a person may register and sit for the written test after completing 17 years of age. If the written test is passed, a learner's permit is issued for a maximum period of 18 months and the holder can practice driving. To face the practical test, the applicant must complete 18 years of age and at least 3 months must have passed after obtaining the learner's permit. Passing the practical test is compulsory. The applicant must also produce an aptitude medical certificate obtained from the National Transport Medical Institution within a period not exceeding 6 months.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka New Driving License
text: For extension of the driving license to heavy vehicles, the applicant must hold a license for light vehicles, specifically only B and B1 vehicle classes, for a minimum of 2 years before the date of application. To obtain a license for light motor bus and motor lorry categories, the minimum height must be 4 feet 10 inches. To obtain a license for motor bus and motor lorry categories, the minimum height must be 5 feet. The applicant may register and sit for the written test after completing 20 years of age. If the written test is passed, a learner's permit is issued for a maximum of 18 months and the holder may practice driving. The applicant must complete 21 years of age to face the practical test and at least 3 months must have passed since obtaining the learner's permit. Passing the practical test is compulsory. An aptitude medical certificate from the National Transport Medical Institution, obtained within 6 months, is required.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Renewal of Validity and Extension of Driving License
section: Renewal of Validity and Extension of Driving License
text: For extension of the validity of a driving license, the page lists these requirements: the current driving license, the national identity card or a valid passport with the national identity card number, and a medical fitness certificate obtained from the Sri Lanka National Transport Institute within a period of six months.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Duplicate Driving License and Change of Particulars
section: Duplicate Driving License and Change of Particulars
text: To obtain a duplicate driving license, the page requires a copy of the complaint lodged at the police within 6 months of the loss of the driving license, the national identity card or a valid passport with the national identity card number, and the distorted or defaced driving license if it is available.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Duplicate Driving License and Change of Particulars
section: Duplicate Driving License and Change of Particulars
text: To change particulars on a driving license, the page requires the current driving license, the national identity card or a valid passport with the national identity card number, a birth certificate, marriage certificate, divorce certificate, or another valid document to support a name change, and a Grama Niladhari certificate for altering the address.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Extension of Driving License
subsection: Including an additional class of vehicles
text: For extension of a driving license by adding an additional class, the page states that the applicant must have the current driving license. The applicant is exempt from the written test when including an additional class of light vehicles, but a holder of a light vehicle license must sit the written test when including a class of heavy vehicles. The requirements for obtaining a new driving license and the requirements for extension to heavy vehicles must be met when including an additional class of light vehicles and a class of heavy vehicles respectively. The page also states that the learner's driving license should be at least 3 months old and the applicant must pass the practical test.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Conversion of Old Driving Licence to New
section: Conversion of Old Driving Licence to New
text: For conversion of an old driving licence to a new one, the page lists these requirements: the current driving license, the national identity card or a valid passport with the national identity card number, and a medical fitness certificate obtained from the National Transport Medical Institute within a period of six months.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Charges For Services
text: Charges for services are governed by the Consolidated Motor Traffic (Driving Licence Levy) Regulations, incorporating Regulations No. 03 of 2022 and amendments up to November 17, 2025. The page states that these may be cited as the Motor Traffic (Driving Licence Levy) Regulations No. 03 of 2022. The levy payable for normal service and same day service is specified in Schedule I, and the levy payable for online service is specified in Schedule II. The Driving Licence (Levy) Regulations No. 01 of 2009 published in Gazette Extraordinary No. 1594/27 of March 26, 2009 are rescinded.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Charges For Services
section: Schedule I - Normal and Same Day Service Levies
text: Schedule I items 1 to 5. Item 1, application for a learner's permit and new driving licence: one class, normal Rs. 2,500 and same day Rs. 3,500; two classes at the same time, normal Rs. 3,000 and same day Rs. 4,000; three or more classes at the same time, normal Rs. 3,500 and same day Rs. 4,500. Item 2, re-applying for the driving licence written test: normal Rs. 500 and no same day levy. Item 3, application for a pair of L plates: size 11 x 9 inches, Rs. 1,000 normal and Rs. 1,000 same day; size 5 x 4 inches, Rs. 500 normal and Rs. 500 same day. Item 4, re-applying for the driving licence practical test: normal Rs. 1,000 and no same day levy. Item 5, adding new classes of vehicles to an existing driving licence: one class, normal Rs. 2,500 and same day Rs. 3,500; two classes at the same time, normal Rs. 3,000 and same day Rs. 4,000; three or more classes at the same time, normal Rs. 3,500 and same day Rs. 4,500.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Charges For Services
section: Schedule I - Normal and Same Day Service Levies
text: Schedule I items 6 to 11. Item 6, renewal or extension of the validity period of a driving licence: for one period, normal Rs. 2,800 and same day Rs. 4,000; additional levy for a lapsed period of one year or less, normal Rs. 500 and no same day levy. Item 7, issuing a duplicate driving licence: for the existing validity period, normal Rs. 2,800 and same day Rs. 4,000; for the existing validity period due to loss of the driving licence, normal Rs. 7,800 and same day Rs. 9,000; additional levy for a lapsed period of one year or less, normal Rs. 500 and no same day levy. Item 8, issuing an extract of driving licence: normal Rs. 1,000 and no same day levy. Item 9, changing the particulars of a driving licence: normal Rs. 3,300 and same day Rs. 4,500. Item 10, changing the particulars of a driving licence at the time of issuing a new driving licence with regard to adding new classes, renewal or extension of validity, or issuing a duplicate under items 5, 6, and 7: additional normal Rs. 500 and same day Rs. 500. Item 11, conversion of the existing driving licence into a new driving licence: normal Rs. 2,800 and same day Rs. 4,000.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Charges For Services
section: Schedule I - Normal and Same Day Service Levies
text: Schedule I items 12 to 16. Item 12, issuing a driving licence to a citizen of Sri Lanka who holds a driving licence or equivalent issued outside Sri Lanka: normal and same day Rs. 30,000; to a person other than a citizen of Sri Lanka holding a foreign driving licence or equivalent: normal and same day Rs. 60,000. Item 13, issuing a driving licence to a non-citizen: normal and same day Rs. 45,000; renewal for a non-citizen: normal and same day Rs. 15,000; duplicate for a non-citizen where the licence is destroyed, defaced, or damaged: normal and same day Rs. 15,000; duplicate for a non-citizen where the licence is lost: normal and same day Rs. 30,000. Item 14, temporary driving licence to a visitor other than a citizen of Sri Lanka: one month Rs. 15,000; more than one month up to two months Rs. 21,000; more than two months up to six months Rs. 30,000; more than six months up to twelve months Rs. 45,000; all at the same rate for normal and same day service. Temporary driving licence to a visitor who is a citizen of Sri Lanka: three months Rs. 15,000; more than three months up to six months Rs. 18,000; more than six months up to twelve months Rs. 24,000; again the same for normal and same day service. Item 15, issuing an international driving permit: normal Rs. 3,000 and same day Rs. 3,500. Item 16, postal charges including packaging, delivery, and courier charges: normal Rs. 225 and no same day levy.

document_title: DMT Sri Lanka Charges For Services
subsection: Online service levy and gazette price revision
text: Schedule II covers levies for online services for Sri Lankan driving licence holders overseas. The listed online service is online verification of the details of a driving licence issued by the Department of Motor Traffic, on an application made by a Sri Lankan citizen overseas by e-mail through the relevant Sri Lankan Embassy or High Commission. The levy is USD 25. The page also links a Gazette - Price Revision reference: No. 2306/02 dated 2022-11-14.
